news.title: Housing supply rises 10% year-on-year, prices up 14% across major cities in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: India’s residential market saw a 10.1% year-on-year increase in supply and a 14.1% rise in property prices in Q1 2026, even as demand moderated sequentially. The shift towards premium housing and larger homes continued, with 2BHK and 3BHK units accounting for the bulk of demand.

news.title: Lucknow Police attaches real-estate-fraud syndicate's assets worth ₹5.78 crore
news.para: Lucknow Police seized assets exceeding Rs 5.78 crore in a major real-estate fraud crackdown. The operation targeted an organized syndicate selling non-existent or disputed land.  The gang specifically preyed on armed forces personnel.  This action under the Gangsters Act aims to curb organized crime and protect citizens. Further seizures are anticipated as investigations continue.

news.title: Illegal colonies pollute Ganga; Haridwar civic body writes to irrigation department
news.para: In localities such as Rajiv Nagar Basti and Sonia Basti, household waste is being discharged into the Ganga, while untreated sewage and wastewater from homes are also flowing directly into the river, significantly increasing pollution levels.

news.title: Chandigarh industrial converted plot owners seek FAR parity
news.para: Converted commercial plot owners in Chandigarh are urging the administration for increased floor area ratio and revised height restrictions.  They highlight substantial financial contributions made during conversion.  The association seeks a clear policy to differentiate commercial and industrial plots.  These changes aim to improve project viability and protect investments.

news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad residents demand action against builders over broken water supply promises
news.para: Housing societies in Pimpri Chinchwad are demanding action against developers who failed to provide water, despite written promises. Residents have spent lakhs on private tankers since 2019. The civic body is compiling a list of defaulting builders, who face legal action and denial of future permissions. Regular supply awaits the Bhama Askhed pipeline completion.

news.title: The Hosteller raises ₹150 crore led by PROMAFT Partners in series B funding
news.para: The Hosteller has raised ₹150 crore in a Series B funding round led by PROMAFT Partners and V3 Ventures, with participation from ITI Growth Opportunities Fund, Merisis Wealth Trust and family offices. The company said the round is the largest institutional fundraise by a backpacker hostel chain in India.

news.title: Lucknow civic body approves major development projects; new township in Amausi included
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation approved significant development proposals, including a township on 21.6 acres in Amausi and handing over land for planned development in Jiyamau. A Rs 1.10 crore ward development fund for each of the 110 wards was also sanctioned for local infrastructure improvements.

news.title: Karnataka RERA disposes of complaints against Nadaprabhu Kempegowda Layout over missing paperwork
news.para: Multiple allottees of Bengaluru's Nadaprabhu Kempegowda Layout project approached the Karnataka Rera against BDA for delayed unit handover. The authority dismissed their complaints, citing a lack of essential documents like sale agreements and payment receipts. Without these, Rera cannot adjudicate claims or calculate delays and interest.

news.title: Higher FAR nods subject to case outcome: Bombay HC
news.para: While granting these approvals, the TCP board should incorporate a clause stating that such applications will be granted subject to the outcome of the petitions, the court said.

news.title: CBI books Ozone Projects, bankers for corruption, cheating homebuyers
news.para: The economic offences wing of the CBI, Delhi, has registered a case against Ozone Projects Pvt Ltd, the developer of Metrozone, its promoters, directors, as well as unknown officials of Axis Bank Ltd and Axis Finance Ltd, on charges of fraud and corruption as per the directions of the Supreme Court.

news.title: DTCP cracks whip on 13 illegal banquet halls, razes hotel in Gurugram
news.para: The banquet halls and marriage venues are in villages like Lohaka, Pahari, Gadaipur, Husainika, Khalilpur, Nanu Kalan, Mahenderwara, Kherla and Jatola. Most of these establishments have been served notices, directing them to cease operations or comply with regulatory norms.

news.title: Ladakh L-G hands over renewed lease deeds to 79 Leh residents
news.para: An official spokesman said the renewal of lease deeds, which were pending for several years, will significantly benefit the residents by ensuring legal ownership and security of tenure.

news.title: Chhattisgarh cabinet halves land registration fees for women to promote economic empowerment
news.para: Chhattisgarh's cabinet has approved a significant 50 percent reduction in land registration charges for properties registered in women's names. This move aims to boost property ownership and economic empowerment for women. Additionally, defence personnel, ex-servicemen, and their widows will receive a 25 percent stamp duty concession on property purchases up to Rs 25 lakh.

news.title: China's new home prices extend decline despite improvement in major cities
news.para: New home prices in China fell 0.2% in March from the previous month, compared with a ​0.3% decline in February. On an annual basis, prices dropped 3.4%, deeper than February's 3.2% fall and the sharpest decline in ten months.

news.title: Home builder sentiment in USA drops to seven-month low in April: NAHB survey
news.para: The National Association of Home Builders/Wells Fargo Housing Market index dropped four points to 34 this month, the lowest level since September 2025, and staying below the 50 break-even point for 24 straight months.

news.title: Switzerland plans stricter rules for real estate purchases by foreigners
news.para: Switzerland is planning to enforce stricter regulations for foreign property buyers. The new measures will particularly impact non-EU/EFTA citizens, limiting their ability to purchase primary homes and commercial properties. This initiative seeks to tackle the ongoing housing crisis and curb rapid population growth.

news.title: SAMHI Hotels signs pact with Ingka Centres for 162-room hotel in Noida
news.para: SAMHI Hotels has entered into a long-term lease agreement with Ingka Centres for a new hotel in Noida, while also approving investments in renewable energy ventures and a subsidiary to support operations and expansion.

news.title: Brigade Group to jointly develop 8.63 acres land in East Bengaluru
news.para: Brigade Group has secured a joint development agreement for a significant land parcel in Gunjur, East Bengaluru. This 8.63-acre plot is a key part of a larger 39-acre residential township. The entire project is estimated to have a gross development value of ₹7,200 crore.

news.title: Office vacancy declines to 13.85% in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: Sustained leasing activity and slower supply additions pushed office vacancy down to 13.85% in Q1 2026, with rentals crossing ₹100 per sq ft per month for the first time.
